halt_stage: CHILD-SIDE FAIL-CLOSED GUARD — _guard_canonical_write at materialize_parquet.py startup
halt_reason: Child process invoked materialize_parquet.py which immediately ran the canonical-write guard (per docs/architecture/manifest-write-flag-spec.md §4 / T002.0a AC-5). The guard requires VESPERA_MANIFEST_COSIGN env var matching ^MC-\d{8}-\d+$ AND VESPERA_MANIFEST_EXPECTED_SHA256 (64-hex or CREATE) before any canonical manifest mutation. Neither env var was set in the wrapper invocation. Guard raised RuntimeError with message 'Refusing to write to canonical data/manifest.csv without VESPERA_MANIFEST_COSIGN=MC-YYYYMMDD-N'. Child exited 11 BEFORE any data load, parquet write, or manifest append occurred.

wrapper_observed.interpretation: Child exited so quickly that wrapper observed only the bare process startup commit (405504 bytes), well below the child's self-reported peak (15.8 MB). This is consistent with a pre-data-load fail-closed exit.

side_effects_assessment: ZERO data-plane side-effects. Wrapper-side artifacts (telemetry CSV, summary JSON, run log) created — these document the failed invocation but do NOT mutate canonical data. Manifest sha256 byte-identical pre/post. memory_budget.py byte-identical. No parquet directory created for May 2025. State materially equivalent to retry-1 ABORT (manifest preserved), with the addition of failed-invocation evidence files.
decision_6_status: NOT_REACHED — child failure prevented validation step.
rollback_executed: NOT_APPLICABLE — zero data-plane side-effects to roll back.

disposition_question.question: Does this child exit 11 from a pre-data-load fail-closed guard (cosign env var missing) consume MC-20260429-1 retry budget, OR is it a pre-Decision-3 gate analogous to R4 / Decision 1 (MC PRESERVED)?
literal_reading_args: Riven ruling §5.1: 'On retry exit ≥ 10 (child failure): MC-29-1 CONSUMED per literal Decision 3 fail criterion.' Exit 11 ≥ 10.
Ruling §3.3 exit-code taxonomy explicitly classifies '≥ 10 (child code)' as 'Decision attempted, failed during execution — disposition' (consumes), distinct from exit 1 (gate).
intent_aligned_args: The cosign guard is a child-side fail-closed PRE-WRITE gate at script startup, structurally analogous to R4/Decision-1 pre-invocation gates: it fires BEFORE any data work and BEFORE any canonical mutation could occur.
Zero data-plane side-effects: parquet not created, manifest byte-identical, no rows appended, no Decision-3 work product attempted.

Ruling §3.2 reasoning: 'The list of trip conditions ... was implicitly scoped to dispositions that occur AS A RESULT OF Decision 3 executing.' The cosign guard fired before Decision-3-class work began, just like R4.

Ruling §3.5 structural argument: 'If R4 firing consumed the MC, R4 would be a worse outcome than no R4 at all.' Same logic applies to the cosign guard — it fired correctly to prevent unsigned canonical mutation, exactly as designed; consuming MC for that would invert the gate's value-add.
Article IV (No Invention): the cosign guard is documented in docs/architecture/manifest-write-flag-spec.md as a fail-closed gate (§4 'guard_canonical_write'); its semantic is gate, not disposition.
disposition_question.operator_observation: The handoff brief did NOT instruct setting VESPERA_MANIFEST_COSIGN or VESPERA_MANIFEST_EXPECTED_SHA256. This is either (a) an operator-error class (Gage should have known to set these — but the brief is silent on them) OR (b) a wrapper-design class (the wrapper could/should propagate the run's MC ID into the env automatically when invoked under MC authority). A separate question for Riven/Aria.
disposition_question.interim_position: MC consumption status PENDING. Retry budget remaining is EITHER 2 (if Riven rules pre-write-guard = gate, MC PRESERVED) OR 1 (if Riven rules literal exit-≥10 = disposition, MC CONSUMED-ONE-SLOT — but note: 'consume MC irrevocably for Jun-2025 as well (no partial continuation)' L2498 implies a single retry consumes BOTH Decision 3 AND Decision 4 slots, not partial). Gage will not retry until ruling issued.

verdict: HALT-REPORT — child exit 11 from cosign-guard pre-write fail-closed; MC consumption disposition AMBIGUOUS pending Riven custodial ruling. Reporting to Orion (aiox-master) for escalation.
mc_consumption_assessment: PENDING — see disposition_question above. Will be set to PRESERVED or CONSUMED-1-SLOT or CONSUMED-IRREVOCABLY based on Riven ruling.
retry_budget_status: PAUSED pending ruling. If gate-class: 2 remaining. If disposition-class: per L2498 'no partial continuation' language, MC fully consumed (Decisions 3+4 both lost) — would require new MC.
next_step_for_orion: Escalate to Riven (@risk-manager) with this evidence file. Request custodial ruling on whether child exit 11 from _guard_canonical_write (pre-data-load, pre-mutation, zero side-effects) is governed by ruling §3.3 exit-code disposition rule (CONSUMES) or by §3.2 'as a result of Decision 3 executing' scope rule (PRESERVED). Separately, Orion should clarify with Riven/Aria whether the wrapper is expected to auto-propagate VESPERA_MANIFEST_COSIGN under MC authority (handoff brief omitted this env var).
next_step_for_gage: STAND BY. Will not retry under MC-29-1 until Riven ruling issued. If Riven rules PRESERVED: retry 3 with cosign env vars exported (VESPERA_MANIFEST_COSIGN=MC-20260429-1, VESPERA_MANIFEST_EXPECTED_SHA256=75e72f2c1185eb795a5db6c5a127706e1b90c30906216a72ea79c443d5391641). If Riven rules CONSUMED: a new MC-YYYYMMDD-N is required; this MC closes.
